exercises.description: Order the sentences (B-G) below to make a story. {v} The first sentence of the story (A) is given for you as an example. {v} A.-Game of Thrones is an American fantasy television series created by David Benioff and D. B. Weiss.
exercises.label: Reading Part 2

questions.title: B The series started on HBO in the United States on April 17, 2011.
C It is an adaptation of A Song of Ice and Fire, George R. R. Martin's series of fantasy novels, the first of which is A Game of Thrones.
D It concluded on May 19, 2019, with 73 episodes and eight seasons.
E The final episode of 'Game of Thrones' was watched by 19.3 million viewers.
F The show was both produced and filmed in Belfast and in the United Kingdom.
G Other filming locations also included Canada, Croatia, Iceland, Malta, Morocco and Spain.

exercises.description: The sentences below are from some instructions, Put these sentences in the right order. The first sentences are done for you {v} A. If IKEA is planning to build a table, they must follow these instructions.

questions.title: B Then they transport the wood to the fabric.
C Transport the table to the store and sell it.
D After that, they can cut the wood and make the pieces of the table.
E Next, they ensemble the pieces.
F Buy enough wood for the table.

exercises.heading: Transport
exercises.description: Person A {v} I’ve always found the topic of traffic to be interesting as it varies in different cities. I think that it’s a topic that requires a lot more attention from the government because, with some simple changes, it could really be improved. Living in Madrid for many years has allowed me to figure out the tricks of avoiding such traffic, but sometimes even leaving home or work just one minute too late can lengthen my commute by thirty minutes or so which leaves me alone with my music. {v} Person B {v} Recently it has made more sense for me to take the metro to work every day instead of driving my car. Traffic was really making my commute extremely long, not to mention that finding a place to leave my car could sometimes take forever. Maybe not so much in smaller cities, but in big cities like Sevilla, we as a community should be giving this a little more thought and find ways to make everyones lives a little bit easier. {v} Person C {v} I have always felt happy about living in an outer suburb of Madrid where I can easily get to and from work using the Renfe or even cycling if I wanted to. Because of this, although I try to empathize with the people who sit in traffic for hours every day, I don’t totally understand how this is. The only experiences I’ve had with big traffic were travelling experiences, and even then I found the best way to pass the time was to turn up the radio and wait. {v} Person D {v} Punctuality is something I live by and so I am always on top of making sure to choose the best form of transportation for the time and time of day. I find that in Barcelona, if I walk out my door at a certain time, I can take my car without a problem. However, if I walk out my door at least five minutes later, my only solution is to hop on the metro. People might think this is a stressful way to live, but I enjoy the constant change.
exercises.subtitle: Four people respond to an article about traffic caused by transportation in cities. Read their comments and answer the questions.
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. Who thinks that there should be more focus on solving the traffic problem in cities?
2. Who explains that a small amount of time can make their drive a lot longer?
3. Who prefers to take public transportation because of difficulty parking?
4. Who thinks that traffic is only a problem in large cities?
5. Who thinks that the best way to handle traffic stress is by listening to music?
6. Who has no problem using different forms of transportation each day?
7. Who makes sure to arrive to work on time every day?

exercises.description: Read the text below. Match the headings A-H to the paragraphs 19-25.
exercises.label: Reading Part 5

answers: H. The results of too much carbon dioxide in water
B. What people are doing to reduce marine pollution
questions.title: 19. Acidification is a type of marine pollution that is caused when the amount of carbon dioxide (CO2) {v} in bodies of water increases. Even though a slight percentage of carbon dioxide is from the {v} environment itself, a higher percentage is contributed by human activities. Over time, the {v} presence of extra carbon dioxide in water leads to increasing the acidity of the water. This results {v} in harming the marine organisms. If acidification is not stopped, it ultimately goes on to {v} negatively affect the food chains that are connected with the water bodies. Coral and plankton do {v} not survive in water that is highly acidic.

answers: E. The effects of excess nutrients
questions.title: 20. When water has too many nutrients, the process is known as eutrophication or hypertrophication. {v} This process encourages the growth of algae and plants in water leading to the reduction of {v} oxygen in water. Shellfish, fish and other aquatic animals find it difficult to survive in water that {v} does not have the appropriate oxygen level. This process is generally caused by the release {v} of fertilizers, detergents containing specific salts, or sewage into the water bodies. The rate at {v} which these nutrients find their way into lakes, seas, or oceans is increasing due to the activities {v} of humans. According to the World Resources Institute, there are 375 coastal zones in the {v} world that are affected by eutrophication.

answers: A. Plastic: the worst cause of marine pollution
questions.title: 21. Plastic is increasingly finding its way into the water bodies. The problem is that plastic never goes {v} away. Moreover, plastic is not only harmful for the life under water, it is a threat to wildlife. {v} Thousands of sea turtles, seals, seabirds and other marine mammals get killed as they eat plastic {v} or get trapped in it. The concentration of plastic pollution is a serious issue for the world’s major {v} oceans - the Atlantic, the Pacific and the Indian ocean. If there is no quick action taken against {v} plastic pollution, it is going to affect the entire marine ecosystem, along with the economy.

answers: F. How plastic waste directly affects humans
questions.title: 22. Additionally, plastic is also harmful to people. Since plastic floats on the surface, it is dangerous {v} for swimmers or surfers. Microplastics are also a threat to humans. They range from the size {v} of a grain of rice to a microscopic sliver, undetectable by the human eye. Thanks to pollution, {v} microplastics find their way into the digestive system of fish and other marine life, and then {v} eventually end up in our food. These plastics, though small, when ingested by humans can have {v} negative effects on our health. They release harmful chemicals into our systems, and though {v} it may be tiny amounts, if enough is ingested over time, it can have serious effects.

answers: C. How dirty water affects more than just fish
questions.title: 23. Toxins, especially those present in industrial waste-water, are one of the main causes of marine {v} pollution. Toxins can also come from agricultural farms and urban areas and they can cause {v} erosion. As toxins enter the water, they lower the level of oxygen content in the water. As most {v} of the toxins easily dissolve with water, it is difficult to separate the toxic waters. With time, the {v} toxins enter the food chain and when humans eat fish from such water, they get ill. Also, there {v} are some animals that feed on fish and they too can be affected by the harmful toxins.

answers: G. What causes underwater noise pollution
questions.title: 24.Underwater noise is generally created by humans and their activities, such as experiments, use {v} of explosives, ship traffic, underwater construction, air guns, and other activities. Though the {v} passage of sound underwater is a topic that humans have studied for centuries, the negative {v} effect of noise on marine life is only recently being discovered, since about the 1990s. And {v} {v} because noise travels very fast in water, scientists are now seeing that the problem of {v} underwater noise pollution for marine life is far greater than that of regular noise pollution on land {v} animals. Marine animals are very sensitive to noise, and studies have shown that certain {v} species will die within a few hours after they have been subjected to a lot of noise pollution.

answers: D. What damage is done by noise pollution
questions.title: 25. Specifically, due to noise pollution from ships, the populations of dolphins and whales have {v} decreased significantly. Squid, cuttlefish, and octopi are also threatened by human-created noise {v} pollution. Thousands of confused, stranded, and injured giant squids turned up in the coastal {v} areas of Spain both in 2001 and 2003, thanks to the use of air guns sending sound across the {v} ocean floor, searching for petroleum. The frequency of the blasts damages, or even fully {v} destroys, the animals’ mechanism that allows them to navigate and maintain balance, both {v} {v} essential for survival. The study of the dead squids confirmed for scientists that noise pollution is {v} a very serious problem that needs to be addressed quickly.

title: Now Streaming
description: Share opinions, describe the people you know, and talk about what you love watching — like a real conversation, not a textbook.
